claimText: The Indian grey wolf occupies a top predator niche in arid and semi-arid open plains and grasslands of Peninsular India.
evidenceExcerpt: Top predator in arid and semi-arid open plains and grasslands of India. Habitat: Peninsular India including Rajasthan, Gujarat, etc.

claimText: The Indian grey wolf is a subspecies of the grey wolf that travels in smaller packs and is less vocal.
evidenceExcerpt: Subspecies of grey wolf; travels in smaller packs and is less vocal.

claimText: The Indian grey wolf is protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA).
The Indian grey wolf faces risks from dog attacks in the Kadbanwadi grassland of Maharashtra.
evidenceExcerpt: Faces risk from dog attacks in Kadbanwadi grassland, Maharashtra.
